summary: Wraps a single-argument callable with bounded memoization so repeated arguments can reuse previously computed return values.
signature_intent: Accepts a unary callable and returns a unary callable with the same argument and return behavior plus memoized reuse.
behavior: Calls the wrapped callable when an argument has not been seen within the retained history.
Returns the previously computed value when the same argument is seen again.
Recognizes matches using strict equality semantics.
Prioritizes recently reused entries for retention.
Discards the least recently retained entry when the configured capacity is exceeded.
edge_cases: Object arguments match only by reference, not by structural equality.
Thrown errors from the wrapped callable are propagated and not retained.
Falsy arguments and falsy return values are handled normally.
If capacity is zero, each call computes fresh but still returns the computed value.
forbidden_details: Source and repository details are intentionally omitted.
Implementation-only ordering mechanics are intentionally omitted.
